title: drymocallis glandulosa var. wrangelliana
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa var. wrangelliana, also known as Wrangell's cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Wrangell's cinquefoil can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health conditions.
